100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On July 14, 1926, the world watched as the British government faced mounting pressure over the ongoing miners' strike, with tensions rising between labor and authorities. In the United States, the Ford Motor Company announced plans to increase production, hinting at a resurgence in the automobile industry. Meanwhile, in Germany, political instability continued as the Weimar Republic grappled with economic challenges and extremist movements. Internationally, the League of Nations sought to mediate disputes in the Middle East, reflecting ongoing geopolitical concerns.
